authoritative_sources.title: Infrastructure Dependency Primer
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the U.S. federal critical-infrastructure risk authority, and its dependency guidance is authoritative for identifying upstream and downstream dependencies when outage, utility, technology, communications, or supplier disruption affects multiple lodging properties.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: portfolio dependency assessment, disruption triage, and cross-property resource prioritization

authoritative_sources.title: Business Continuity Planning
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FEMA's Ready.gov guidance is an official public continuity-planning baseline for identifying critical operations, dependencies, recovery priorities, and continuity constraints that shape portfolio disruption summaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: continuity planning, recovery-priority framing, and disruption escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Hospitality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OpenTravel is a recognized travel technology standards body, and its hospitality specifications define public booking, availability, reservation, and property-information vocabulary used to keep PMS and CRS rollup evidence consistent across properties.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: booking, availability, reservation, PMS rollup, and property artifact normalization

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official short-term lodging guidance constrains portfolio actions that would change advertised price, mandatory fee, fee description, cancellation, or refundability treatment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pricing, refund, fee, cancellation, and policy-exception boundary handling
used_for: pricing and fee override refusal triggers
portfolio impact packaging for revenue owners

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for credit-card billing-error guidance and is authoritative for merchant-versus-issuer boundaries relevant to folio mismatches, duplicate charges, and refund-not-posted complaints during disruptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: folio mismatch, duplicate charge, refund-not-posted, and payment boundary escalation
used_for: billing-error routing instead of portfolio override

authoritative_sources.title: Travel-associated Outbreaks as a Public Health Issue
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the U.S. public-health authority, and this accommodation-specific outbreak guidance directly affects guest-list handling, incoming or past guest notification, and escalation behavior after suspected exposure events at lodging properties.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: public-health-sensitive disruption, guest movement, and notification escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Hotel and Motel Fire Safety Flyer
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USFA is a federal fire-safety authority, and its hotel and motel guidance is authoritative for safety-sensitive guest movement, fire alarm, evacuation, and unavailable-room situations that portfolio operations must escalate rather than clear.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fire, evacuation, unavailable-room, and safety-sensitive guest relocation handling

authoritative_sources.title: Travel Scams
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC consumer fraud guidance is authoritative for suspicious travel booking and payment patterns that require fraud or loss-prevention escalation instead of portfolio operations handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fraud-sensitive booking, payment, refund reroute, and suspicious-identity handling

authoritative_sources.title: Safe Stay
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHLA is the principal U.S. hotel industry association, and Safe Stay is a public hotel safety, security, and health initiative that frames recognized lodging operating expectations and safety-resource routing for hotels.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lodging safety, security, health-resource routing, and guest-impact escalation framing

workflow_stages.name: Portfolio intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm property set, planning horizon, request intent, tenant operating model, service tier, authority map, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, approval-bound, safety-sensitive, fraud-sensitive, or adjacent-lane-owned.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Record aggregation and artifact normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize PMS rollups, booking and availability records, room-status summaries, staffing rosters, issue trackers, outage logs, incident notes, folio or payment signals, and guest-impact records into a portfolio operations frame.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess occupancy, arrivals, departures, out-of-order rooms, clean-room availability, labor pool, call-offs, overtime or scheduling limits, service-speed targets, dependencies, outages, and incident constraints before recommending allocation or sequence changes.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Recommendation-only allocation and sequencing
workflow_stages.description: Prepare staffing, overflow, priority, or sequencing options using documented tenant rules and traceable evidence without claiming final budget, labor, room-control, pricing, refund, or executive approval authority.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: High-volume intake and disruption summary
workflow_stages.description: Group guest and operational impact by property, issue class, system state, service standard, safety or fraud signal, and next owner while preserving source records and missing-evidence blockers.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Policy-exception, safety, fraud, and approval escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop unsupported action and package escalation when pricing, refunds, compensation, safety clearance, fraud, public health, emergency response, labor, budget, or executive approval determines the answer.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: portfolio operations dashboard snapshots, property identifiers, property tier or classification, and operating model
PMS and CRS rollups, occupancy forecast, arrivals and departures, house count, group block pickup, overbooking and walk-risk reports
room-status summaries, out-of-order room counts, housekeeping queue status, clean-room availability, and maintenance blockers
staffing rosters, call-off reports, floating labor-pool rules, overtime thresholds, property labor constraints, and third-party staffing limits
service-level or service-speed standards, tenant allocation rules, priority tiers, owner approval matrix, and escalation directory
CRM records, guest-impact lists, service tickets, contact-center queues, issue tracker records, outage logs, incident summaries, and emergency or safety status
folio, payment, refund, cancellation, fee, compensation, and fraud or loss-prevention references when customer-impact exceptions affect portfolio decisions

allowed: Classify the request as in scope, out of scope, blocked, approval-bound, safety-sensitive, fraud-sensitive, or adjacent-lane-owned.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, property records, authoritative source context, and retrieval hooks.
Assess cross-property constraints, capacity, dependencies, demand, staffing, room status, and guest-impact evidence.
Prepare recommendation-only staffing, overflow, priority, and sequencing packages using documented allocation rules.
Produce cross-property allocation briefs, portfolio disruption summaries, constraint logs, and escalation notes.
escalate: Required PMS rollup, staffing, room-status, issue-tracker, incident, payment, policy, or approval records are missing or materially conflict.
The recommendation depends on final budget, labor, executive, pricing, refund, compensation, room-control, safety, public-health, fraud, legal, or policy-override authority.
Customer harm, guest relocation, public health, fire, security, fraud, or suspicious payment signals change the safe answer.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, property, owner, labor, supplier, processor, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Approve overtime spend, staffing changes, guest walks, room-control moves, service-level cuts, or executive tradeoffs as final decisions.
Override pricing, refund, cancellation, fee, compensation, service-recovery, safety, fraud, or accessibility policy without explicit authority.
Treat a single-property execution request as owned portfolio operations work.
Move guests, staff, or work across properties when safety, fraud, labor, policy, or source-record blockers remain unresolved.

domain_failure_modes: the lane presents a staffing, overflow, guest-walk, service-level, or budget recommendation as a final approval
a single-property front-office, housekeeping, revenue, fraud, safety, or guest-service task is absorbed into portfolio operations without the right owner
PMS rollups, labor records, room-status summaries, issue trackers, or incident records conflict but the lane still recommends deterministic reallocation
demand exceeds capacity and the lane invents a priority tie-break instead of using documented tenant allocation rules or escalating
a property-level pricing, refund, cancellation, fee, compensation, or service-recovery exception is applied across the portfolio without approval
fire, water intrusion, public-health, security, or fraud signals are treated as routine staffing or overflow problems
high-volume outage intake is flattened into generic ticket triage without property, guest-impact, record-status, or next-owner segmentation
tenant-specific adaptation loses the public safety, fraud, pricing, refund, and consumer-protection baseline

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Demand, outage, or staffing disruption requires cross-property reallocation
One property requests a local exception that creates portfolio-level impact
A decision appears to require budget or executive approval outside the lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific lodging, labor scheduling, fire safety, public-health, fee-disclosure, cancellation, and refund rules can materially narrow portfolio recommendations.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, property procedures, labor rules, fee and refund policy, and applicable safety or public-health instructions before applying property-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Owner agreements, brand standards, union or local scheduling limits, third-party staffing contracts, and property emergency plans may assign final authority differently by tenant or property.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane records the ambiguity and routes to the designated approver or adjacent specialist instead of guessing. No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the package requires explicit authority and escalation paths.
